articles.headline: Cabinet approves 12 industrial smart cities with ₹28,602 crore investment
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet has cleared the development of 12 new industrial smart cities across 10 states under the National Industrial Corridor Development Programme. The project involves an investment of ₹28,602 crore and aims to create a robust industrial backbone. It is expected to generate 1 million direct and 3 million indirect jobs while boosting the domestic manufacturing sector.

articles.headline: Reliance Industries to consider 1:1 bonus share issue on September 5
articles.summary: Reliance Industries Limited (RIL) announced that its board will meet on September 5 to consider a 1:1 bonus share issue. This would be the company's first bonus issue since 2017. The announcement was made ahead of the company's 47th Annual General Meeting. Markets reacted positively, with RIL shares leading gains in the Sensex and Nifty indices following the news.

articles.headline: Cabinet approves ₹3,300 crore Kaynes semiconductor plant in Gujarat
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet has approved Kaynes Semicon's proposal to establish a semiconductor unit in Sanand, Gujarat. The project involves an investment of ₹3,302 crore and will have a production capacity of 6.3 million chips per day. This marks the fifth semiconductor plant approved under the India Semiconductor Mission, strengthening the nation's electronics manufacturing ecosystem.

articles.headline: Union Cabinet clears three railway projects worth ₹6,456 crore
articles.summary: The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs approved three railway projects totaling ₹6,456 crore to improve connectivity in Odisha, Jharkhand, West Bengal, and Chhattisgarh. The projects include new lines and multi-tracking to facilitate the transport of minerals, agricultural products, and passengers. The projects are slated for completion within a four-year timeframe.

articles.headline: India Q1 GDP growth likely slowed to 6.8% due to spending
articles.summary: Economic analysts expect India's GDP growth for the April-June quarter of FY25 to slow to approximately 6.8-7% from 7.8% in the previous quarter. The slowdown is attributed to reduced government capital expenditure during the general elections and a slight dip in manufacturing activity. Official data from the NSO is scheduled for release this Friday.

articles.headline: Nifty settles at record high for eighth consecutive session
articles.summary: The NSE Nifty 50 reached a new all-time high, closing at 25,052.35 points. This marks the eighth consecutive day of gains, driven by strong buying in IT and pharmaceutical stocks. Reliance Industries and HDFC Bank provided significant support to the rally. The BSE Sensex also gained 73 points to end the session at 81,785.56.

articles.headline: Zomato to replace JSW Steel in BSE Sensex from September 23
articles.summary: Food delivery giant Zomato is set to join the benchmark BSE Sensex starting September 23, replacing JSW Steel. This inclusion marks the first time a new-age tech firm will be part of the 30-stock index. The rebalancing reflects the platform's growing market capitalization. Analysts expect significant passive inflows into Zomato shares following the inclusion.

articles.headline: Paris 2024 Paralympics opening ceremony begins with grand display
articles.summary: The Paris 2024 Paralympic Games officially commenced with a grand opening ceremony at the Place de la Concorde. India has sent its largest-ever contingent of 84 athletes to compete in 12 sports disciplines. The team aims to surpass its previous record medal haul from Tokyo. Sumit Antil and Bhagyashri Jadhav served as flag bearers during the parade.

articles.headline: Government notifies Unified Pension Scheme rules for central employees
articles.summary: The Centre has officially notified the Unified Pension Scheme (UPS) effective from April 1, 2025. The scheme guarantees a pension of 50% of the average basic pay for employees with at least 25 years of service. It also includes family pensions and a minimum monthly pension of ₹10,000. Over 2.3 million central government employees are expected to benefit.

articles.headline: Vodafone Idea in talks with banks for ₹30,000 crore loan
articles.summary: Vodafone Idea is reportedly in advanced discussions with a consortium of banks, led by State Bank of India, to secure a loan of approximately ₹30,000 crore. The funds are intended for capital expenditure to expand 4G coverage and roll out 5G services. The telco is seeking to improve its competitive position against rivals Bharti Airtel and Reliance Jio.

articles.headline: India Looking to Trim Non-Essential Imports to Support Rupee
articles.summary: The Indian government is identifying non-essential items and goods where import dependence is low to potentially impose higher duties or selective restrictions. The move follows a surge in the merchandise trade deficit to $19.1 billion in April and is aimed at curbing rupee depreciation and encouraging domestic manufacturing capacity as part of a review of current trade policies.

articles.headline: Sacked H-1B Staff Asked to Do More B-2 Paperwork in US
articles.summary: Laid-off H-1B visa holders in the US are facing increased scrutiny and requests for additional documentation as they try to transition to B-2 visitor status to stay in the country. Legal experts report a rise in Requests for Evidence (RFEs) and Notices of Intent to Deny from US authorities, making it harder for tech workers to find new employment or manage their relocation effectively.

articles.headline: I Squared Capital Bets $1 Billion on Indian Power Transmission Assets
articles.summary: US-based private equity firm I Squared Capital has launched a $1-billion platform to invest in India's power transmission sector. The firm aims to capitalize on the country's need for infrastructure to build a modern and efficient national grid. The new entity, named Cube Grid, will focus on acquiring and developing greenfield and brownfield projects over the next several years.

articles.headline: INOXGFL Emerges as Front-runner to Acquire 1.1 GW Vena Energy India
articles.summary: INOXGFL Group has become the leading contender to acquire the 1.1 GW renewable energy business of Vena Energy India. The deal is estimated to have an enterprise value of approximately ₹4,500 crore to ₹5,000 crore. The acquisition would significantly expand INOXGFL's renewable energy platform, which currently includes projects across wind and solar energy sectors in India.

articles.headline: Banks Urge RBI to Relax Lending Norms for Infrastructure InvITs
articles.summary: Indian banks have requested the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) to ease proposed lending norms for Infrastructure Investment Trusts (InvITs). Lenders argue that the current three-year operational track record requirement could slow down essential infrastructure monetization and choke funding for projects. The plea seeks more flexible rules to ensure consistent capital flow into the sector.

articles.headline: Centre Plans 5,000 Stations for E100 Flex Fuel Dispensing
articles.summary: The Indian government is working on a plan to set up 5,000 specialized stations to dispense E100 flex fuel within the next two years. This initiative, announced by the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas, aims to reduce the country's dependence on crude oil imports and promote cleaner energy alternatives for the transport sector. Major oil marketing companies will lead the rollout.

articles.headline: Government Considers Translating Legal Rules into Automated Software Codes
articles.summary: The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) is exploring a 'law-to-code' solution to translate legal provisions into automated software rules. The project aims to ensure easier compliance with digital laws, such as the DPDP Act, and help organizations automatically detect and prevent cyber threats. The move is part of a broader effort to modernize India's legal framework.

articles.headline: More Companies Opt for Confidential Route in IPO Filings
articles.summary: An increasing number of Indian companies are choosing the confidential filing route for their Initial Public Offerings (IPOs) to manage market volatility. This method allows firms to submit a draft prospectus to SEBI without disclosing sensitive financial details to the public immediately. Experts suggest this provides greater flexibility in timing the launch based on market conditions.

articles.headline: Election Commission Considers Monthly Voter Registration for 18-Year-Olds
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India (ECI) is deliberating on a proposal to allow those turning 18 to apply for voter registration every month. Currently, there are four fixed cut-off dates annually. The move aims to ensure more young citizens are included in the electoral rolls and to streamline the process for continuous revision, reducing the wait time for first-time voters.

articles.headline: New CAA Rules: Applicants Must Declare Passport and Foreign Status
articles.summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs has notified amendments to the Citizenship Amendment Act (CAA) Rules, 2024. Applicants seeking Indian citizenship are now required to declare if they are in possession of a foreign passport and disclose details of their previous citizenship status. The move is intended to ensure transparency and prevent issues related to dual citizenship under the new law.

articles.headline: US Clears $428 Million Support Package for India’s Howitzers and Helicopters
articles.summary: The United States government has approved two military support packages for India valued at $428 million. The deal includes sustainment support for M777 ultra-lightweight howitzers and AH-64E Apache attack helicopters. The support is intended to enhance India's capability to meet current and future threats and strengthen the strategic defense partnership between the two nations.

articles.headline: India to Support Vietnam in Modernising its Defence Forces
articles.summary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh has reiterated India's commitment to supporting the modernization of Vietnam’s defense forces. During a bilateral meeting, both sides discussed enhancing cooperation in areas like maritime security, joint exercises, and defense research. The move is part of India's broader strategy to strengthen ties with Southeast Asian nations in the Indo-Pacific region.

articles.headline: Economic Blockade in Manipur Continues Amid Hostage Crisis
articles.summary: A major economic blockade continues on national highways in Manipur, leading to severe shortages of food, fuel, and essential supplies. The blockade was initiated by Kuki and Naga groups demanding the release of hostages following a recent flare-up in regional tensions. Local authorities and community leaders are engaged in talks to resolve the crisis and restore normalcy to the state.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi Warns of Economic Storm Impacting Small Businesses
articles.summary: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi has warned of a 'ferocious economic storm' that he says will hit ordinary people and small businesses in India. He criticized the government’s economic policies, alleging they favor large conglomerates at the expense of the middle class and workers. Gandhi urged for a structural change in the economy to protect vulnerable sections from impending financial distress.

articles.headline: Karnataka CM’s Aides Meet Minister Jarkiholi Amid Leadership Speculation
articles.summary: Speculation over a potential leadership change in Karnataka has intensified after four close aides of Chief Minister Siddaramaiah met with State Minister Satish Jarkiholi. The meeting has triggered discussions within the ruling Congress party regarding future political alignments. While leaders have termed it a routine visit, political analysts are watching the developments closely.

articles.headline: Debate Over Namaz on Roads Resurfaces in Uttar Pradesh
articles.summary: The political debate over offering Namaz on public roads has resurfaced in Uttar Pradesh after remarks from senior BJP and Samajwadi Party leaders. Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath emphasized that religious activities should not disrupt public life, while opposition leaders countered that appropriate spaces should be provided for prayers. The issue continues to be a point of contention in the state.

articles.headline: India and Italy Collaborate on Indo-Mediterranean Strategic Vision
articles.summary: India and Italy have outlined a joint vision for the 'Indo-Mediterranean' region, focusing on maritime security, trade corridors, and energy cooperation. The partnership aims to enhance connectivity between the two regions through infrastructure projects and digital networks. Both nations emphasized the importance of a rules-based international order and stability in the strategic corridor.

articles.headline: Kerala Government to Review State’s Financial Health with Special Panel
articles.summary: The Kerala cabinet has decided to form a special panel to prepare a white paper on the state’s financial situation. The committee, comprising experts and senior officials, will examine the fiscal health, debt levels, and revenue sources of the state. The move comes amid allegations of fiscal mismanagement and is aimed at suggesting measures for financial stability and better resource planning.

articles.headline: Chemists in Karnataka to Hold 24-Hour Shutdown Over E-Pharmacy Issues
articles.summary: Nearly 26,000 chemists across Karnataka are set to participate in a 24-hour shutdown to protest against the regulation of e-pharmacies and online medicine sales. The protest, organized by the AIOCD, aims to highlight concerns over the impact of corporate platforms on traditional retail pharmacies and the risks of unregulated medicine sales. Essential medicine supplies will be affected during this period.

articles.headline: Cement Companies Diverge on Capex Plans Amid West Asia Crisis
articles.summary: Large Indian cement manufacturers are adopting contrasting strategies regarding capital expenditure due to the ongoing West Asia conflict and fluctuating input costs. While some firms are calibrating their near-term investments to preserve cash, others like UltraTech continue to move forward with expansion plans. The sector is navigating a period of uncertainty while preparing for long-term demand growth.

articles.headline: Intuit Signs ₹915-crore Lease for Large Office Space in Bengaluru
articles.summary: Financial software giant Intuit has signed a significant lease deal worth ₹915 crore for 630,000 square feet of office space in Bengaluru. The expansion is part of the company’s plan to boost its research and development operations in India. The deal reflects continued demand for premium commercial real estate from global technology firms despite shifting work models across the industry.

articles.headline: Tata Steel UK Secures Exemption from US Steel Tariffs
articles.summary: Tata Steel UK has been granted an exemption from US tariffs on certain steel products, providing a boost to its international operations. The exemption applies to specific high-grade steel used in various industries, allowing the company to compete more effectively in the American market. The move is seen as a positive development for the company amid global trade challenges and rising costs.

articles.headline: Prudential May Seek More Time to Reduce Stake in ICICI Venture
articles.summary: Global insurer Prudential is likely to seek additional time from regulators to reduce its stake in its joint venture with ICICI Bank. Current regulations require foreign partners to bring down their holding to a specified limit within a set timeframe. The company is evaluating its options to comply with the rules while ensuring a smooth transition and maintaining its long-term market presence.

articles.headline: HDFC Bank Implements Two-Day WFH for Non-customer Facing Staff
articles.summary: HDFC Bank has announced a new work-from-home policy, allowing non-customer facing employees to work remotely for up to two days a week. The move is aimed at enhancing employee well-being and flexibility while maintaining operational efficiency. The bank joins several other large corporate entities in adopting a hybrid work model following shifts in workplace dynamics in the post-pandemic era.

articles.headline: Zee Entertainment Reports ₹104 Crore Net Loss in Fourth Quarter
articles.summary: Zee Entertainment Enterprises reported a consolidated net loss of ₹104 crore for the quarter ended March 2026. The loss was attributed to higher investments in digital content and a dip in advertising revenues. Despite the quarterly loss, the company is focusing on strategic restructuring and content diversification to drive future growth and regain its market position in the media sector.

articles.headline: PepsiCo Plans ₹5,700 Crore Investment to Expand India Manufacturing
articles.summary: PepsiCo has announced a major investment plan of ₹5,700 crore to expand its manufacturing footprint in India by 2030. The investment will focus on building new units and scaling up existing facilities to meet the growing demand for its snacks and beverage portfolio. The move underscores India's importance as a key growth market for the global FMCG giant.

articles.headline: Bayer Seeks to Deepen Presence in India’s Self-Care Market
articles.summary: Global pharmaceutical major Bayer is betting big on India's self-care and wellness market, with plans to launch a series of new products. The company aims to expand its reach to millions of households by focusing on digestive health and preventive care. Bayer sees significant potential for growth in India as consumers increasingly prioritize health and wellness in their daily routines.

articles.headline: Advent International Buys Minority Stake in Iscon Balaji Foods for $150m
articles.summary: Private equity firm Advent International has acquired a minority stake in Indian potato processing company Iscon Balaji Foods for $150 million. The investment will help the company expand its production capacity and strengthen its presence in both domestic and international markets. Iscon Balaji Foods is a major supplier of frozen snacks and dehydrated potato products.

articles.headline: Airtel Introduces 5G Network Slicing for Postpaid Customers in India
articles.summary: Bharti Airtel has launched 5G network slicing for its postpaid customers, a first in the Indian market. This technology allows the operator to create multiple virtual networks on a single physical 5G infrastructure, offering dedicated bandwidth and lower latency for specific user groups or applications. The move is aimed at enhancing the digital experience for premium and enterprise users.

articles.headline: Mystery Surrounds Anonymous X Account Supporting Elon Musk
articles.summary: A social media account on X, formerly Twitter, has gained widespread attention for its consistent and vocal support of Elon Musk. The anonymous account, which frequently amplifies Musk's views and attacks his critics, has sparked curiosity about its true identity. While some speculate on its origins, the account's significant influence has made it a focal point of discussion in tech circles.

articles.headline: Jasprit Bumrah and Ravindra Jadeja Rested for Afghanistan Test
articles.summary: India's star players Jasprit Bumrah and Ravindra Jadeja have been rested for the upcoming one-off Test match against Afghanistan. The selection committee cited workload management as the primary reason for the decision, especially with a busy international schedule ahead. In their absence, several younger players are expected to get an opportunity to showcase their talent in the national side.

articles.headline: Indian Liquor Companies Reposition Brandy as an Aspirational Spirit
articles.summary: Major liquor manufacturers in India are rebranding brandy to transform its image into an aspirational and premium spirit. Historically considered a mass-market product, companies are now launching high-end variants and targeting younger consumers through sophisticated marketing and packaging. The shift aims to capture a larger share of the growing premium alcohol segment in the country.

articles.headline: Amul Strategy Focuses on Frozen and Organic Food Expansion
articles.summary: Dairy giant Amul is diversifying its portfolio by aggressively expanding into frozen foods, organic products, and health-focused beverages. The cooperative aims to leverage its vast distribution network to reach a wider consumer base and compete with global FMCG firms. The strategy includes the launch of new products like frozen pizza, protein shakes, and organic grains across India.

articles.headline: Meta and Google Face Criticism Over Kids’ Social Media Addiction
articles.summary: Tech giants Meta and Google are facing renewed backlash from advocates and regulators over the design of their platforms, which critics argue promotes social media addiction among children. The concerns center on features that encourage prolonged usage and the impact on youth mental health. Both companies are under pressure to introduce more robust safeguards and transparency measures.

articles.headline: WHO Warns Against Sugarcoated Marketing of Nicotine Products to Youth
articles.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) has issued a warning against the 'sugarcoated' marketing tactics used by companies to sell nicotine and tobacco products to young people. The global body called for stricter regulations on flavored products and social media promotions that target minors. WHO urged governments to take immediate action to protect future generations from nicotine addiction.

articles.headline: Trump warns of strikes as Iran threatens to open new fronts
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump warned that military strikes against Iran could resume within days despite ongoing negotiations. Trump stated he had previously held off an assault in hopes of a peace deal. In response, Iran's army spokesman Mohammad Akraminia warned that the Islamic Republic would open new fronts against the US if attacks were relaunched.

articles.headline: Delhi court denies interim bail to former JNU student Umar Khalid
articles.summary: A city court dismissed the interim bail plea of Umar Khalid, an accused in the 2020 Delhi riots case. Khalid had sought 15 days of bail to attend his uncle's post-death rituals and care for his mother during surgery. The court described the grounds for the petition as unreasonable. Khalid is currently facing charges under the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act.

articles.headline: TMC candidate Jahangir Khan withdraws from Falta assembly repoll contest
articles.summary: Jahangir Khan, the Trinamool Congress candidate for Falta, withdrew from the race two days before the scheduled repoll. Khan cited the interest of the constituency following Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari’s promise of a special development package. The Election Commission had ordered a fresh poll due to severe electoral offenses recorded during the initial voting phase.

articles.headline: Kolkata Municipal Corporation issues notices to properties linked to Abhishek Banerjee
articles.summary: The Kolkata Municipal Corporation served notices to buildings linked to TMC MP Abhishek Banerjee’s company, Leaps and Bounds Private Limited. The notices, issued under Section 400(1) of the KMC Act, allege illegal construction in certain portions of the buildings. Officials described the move as a standard protocol preceding potential demolition drives.

articles.headline: Education ministry defends VBSA Bill over status of state universities
articles.summary: The education ministry informed a parliamentary committee that the proposed Viksit Bharat Shiksha Adhishthan (VBSA) Bill will not alter the governance of state-affiliated universities. The bill allows institutions to open offshore centers subject to approval. The ministry argued that the law supplements rather than replaces state government authority in the higher education sector.

articles.headline: DGCA officials to observe Boeing fuel control switch testing in Seattle
articles.summary: India's Directorate General of Civil Aviation is sending a team to Boeing’s Seattle facility to observe the testing of a fuel control switch. The component was removed from an Air India Boeing 787 following a defect reported at London's Heathrow airport in February. Investigators are specifically focusing on the switch's locking mechanism following recent safety concerns.

articles.headline: Transporters in Delhi-NCR to begin three-day strike starting May 21
articles.summary: The All India Motor Transport Congress (AIMTC) has called for a three-day strike across the Delhi-NCR region starting May 21. Over 68 transport unions are protesting the recent hike in environment compensation cess on commercial vehicles and a proposed ban on older vehicles. The suspension of operations is expected to impact logistics and the supply of essential goods.

articles.headline: Man dies after setting himself on fire outside wife’s house
articles.summary: A 29-year-old man died in Delhi's Jahangirpuri after setting himself on fire outside his estranged wife's home following a domestic dispute. Before his death, the man shared a video on social media accusing his wife and mother-in-law of harassment. Police are investigating the incident based on the social media footage and statements from the families.

articles.headline: Google revamps search engine with AI agents and new coding tools
articles.summary: Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai unveiled a new design for Google Search at the I/O 2026 conference. The update features AI agents capable of handling complex queries, making reservations, and monitoring health. The firm also launched 'Antigravity,' a platform for AI-assisted coding, as it competes with rivals like OpenAI and Anthropic in the lucrative developer market.

articles.headline: Manufacturing sector relative share in India's GDP continues to decline
articles.summary: Analysis of UNCTAD data shows India’s manufacturing share of global GDP fell to 2.98% in 2024, despite a 3.54% overall GDP share. The sector's share in gross capital formation dropped below 20% in 2023-24 from a peak of 45% in the mid-1990s. Experts link the high merchandise trade deficit to this manufacturing underperformance and high import-dependence for value addition.

articles.headline: Supreme Court permits euthanasia for rabid and dangerous stray dogs
articles.summary: The Supreme Court refused to modify its 2025 order to remove stray dogs from public institutional areas like schools and hospitals. For the first time, the bench explicitly permitted the euthanasia of rabid, incurably ill, or demonstrably dangerous dogs following veterinary assessment. Animal welfare groups must now file liability affidavits to feed dogs on institutional campuses.

articles.headline: IMD issues orange alerts as Delhi heatwave hits 45.1 degrees Celsius
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued orange alerts for the next six days as temperatures in Delhi peaked at 45.1°C on Tuesday. In Banda, Uttar Pradesh, the mercury reached a record 48.2°C. The heatwave has led to a 75% increase in fire calls in the capital and forced the CAQM to impose Stage 1 GRAP measures to combat deteriorating air quality.

articles.headline: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio to visit India on May 23
articles.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is scheduled to visit India from May 23-26. The visit includes stops in Kolkata, Agra, Jaipur, and New Delhi. Rubio will participate in a Quad foreign ministers' meeting and hold bilateral talks on security, trade, and energy. The trip aims to deepen the strategic partnership amid shifting geopolitical dynamics in the Indo-Pacific.

articles.headline: Amit Shah outlines roadmap for Bastar development and Maoist eradication
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah detailed a plan to transform Bastar into a model of prosperity within five years. The strategy includes converting 70 security camps into development hubs to provide banking, health, and education services. Shah emphasized that the region's development was previously hindered by Maoist violence and promised a sixfold increase in local average income.

articles.headline: Congo Ebola outbreak deaths rise to 134 as virus spreads
articles.summary: The World Health Organization expressed concern over a rare Bundibugyo Ebola virus outbreak in eastern Congo. Suspected deaths have risen to at least 134, with over 500 suspected cases reported. The virus, which lacks approved vaccines, spread undetected for weeks. The US and Germany are taking precautions after an American doctor in the region contracted the disease.

articles.headline: Carlos Alcaraz withdraws from Wimbledon due to persistent wrist injury
articles.summary: Defending champion Carlos Alcaraz has pulled out of Wimbledon and the Queen’s Club tournament as he continues to recover from a wrist injury. The Spaniard sustained the injury during the Barcelona Open and has missed several major tournaments including Roland Garros. Alcaraz stated that despite improvement, he is not yet ready for competitive play at the All England Club.

articles.headline: Two tigers found dead with limbs severed in Rajaji Tiger Reserve
articles.summary: Carcasses of two young male tigers with severed limbs were discovered in the Haridwar forest division. Forest officials arrested a 37-year-old man, Alam, while his accomplice fled the scene. The tigers were allegedly poisoned after feeding on a buffalo carcass sprayed with toxins. A Ninth tiger recently died in the Kanha Range, suspected to be caused by canine distemper virus.

articles.headline: Mango fashion group heir Jonathan Andic arrested in homicide probe
articles.summary: Jonathan Andic, son of Mango founder Isak Andic, was arrested in Barcelona as a suspect in his father's death. Isak Andic died in a 2024 cliff-top fall originally treated as an accident. A judge set bail at 1 million euros and barred Jonathan from leaving the region. The family maintains his innocence, claiming there is no legitimate evidence linking him to a possible homicide.

articles.headline: WHO pandemic pact negotiations remain deadlocked over pathogen sharing
articles.summary: Negotiations for a global Pandemic Agreement reached an impasse at the World Health Assembly in Geneva. Member states are divided on the Pathogen Access and Benefit Sharing (PABS) system. Developing nations are demanding enforceable contracts to ensure they receive vaccines and treatments in exchange for sharing genetic data, a move resisted by some developed-country blocs.

articles.headline: Pep Guardiola to leave Manchester City after ten successful seasons
articles.summary: Pep Guardiola is reportedly set to depart Manchester City at the end of the 2025-26 campaign. During his ten-year tenure, Guardiola led the club to 20 trophies, including six Premier League titles and a Champions League victory. Enzo Maresca, a former member of Guardiola’s coaching staff, is considered the leading candidate to succeed him as manager.

articles.headline: Karnataka High Court stays planned strike by transport workers' unions
articles.summary: A vacation bench of the Karnataka High Court temporarily halted a statewide bus strike scheduled for Wednesday. The court directed the state government to hold discussions with union representatives regarding demands for a 25% wage hike. The interim order followed a petition arguing that a shutdown would cause widespread hardship to commuters relying on public transport.

articles.headline: Over twenty thousand pharmacists to join nationwide shutdown on May 20
articles.summary: More than 20,000 pharmacists across Karnataka will participate in a 24-hour nationwide strike called by the AIOCD. The protest targets the growth of e-pharmacies and alleged regulatory violations. While pharmacies will be shut, the association stated it had informed customers in advance. Emergency medicine supplies may face disruptions during the protest period.

articles.headline: NRI arrested in Bengaluru for illegal possession of live ammunition
articles.summary: A 34-year-old US-based NRI, Arman Mutaahar, was arrested in Bengaluru after hotel staff found live ammunition in his room. The discovery was made at the Jayamahal Palace Hotel after the suspect had vacated his room. Police have registered a case under the Arms Act and are investigating how the ammunition was brought into the country without proper documentation.

articles.headline: Joint Parliamentary Committee on ‘One Nation, One Election’ meets Padma awardees
articles.summary: The JPC on the ‘One Nation, One Election’ initiative held consultative meetings with Padma awardees in Bengaluru to gather civic perspectives. Chairman P.P. Chaudhary stated the goal is to represent citizen interests over political ones. While some attendees praised the exercise for including diverse views, opposition leaders in Karnataka continue to reject the proposal as federal overreach.

articles.headline: Mallikarjun Kharge slams Prime Minister over rising fuel and fertilizer prices
articles.summary: Congress President Mallikarjun Kharge criticized the Central government for its failure to control inflation and the depreciation of the rupee. Kharge warned that the exchange rate could hit 100 rupees per dollar, leading to an 'economic storm.' He accused Prime Minister Narendra Modi of lacking the foresight to navigate global crises and failing to protect farmers and the youth.

articles.headline: Dubare elephant camp shut temporarily following tourist death and elephant fight
articles.summary: Karnataka's forest department closed the Dubare Elephant Camp for two days after a tourist was killed during a fight between two elephants. One of the elephants involved, 28-year-old Martanda, died from deep internal tusk injuries. Minister Eshwar Khandre has ordered new safety protocols, requiring visitors to maintain a minimum distance of 100 feet from the animals.

articles.headline: Supreme Court sentences nine to life for murder of Chennai doctor
articles.summary: The Supreme Court reversed a Madras High Court acquittal and sentenced nine people to life imprisonment for the 2013 murder of Dr. Subbiah. The bench criticized the High Court for entertaining 'vague doubts' in a well-reasoned case. Seven convicts were ordered to surrender within two weeks, while the sentences for an elderly couple were suspended pending a pardon plea.

articles.headline: Andhra SIT charges former minister Jogi Ramesh in counterfeit liquor case
articles.summary: The Special Investigation Team (SIT) of Andhra Pradesh police filed a 1,300-page chargesheet against 25 people, including YSRCP leader Jogi Ramesh. The case involves the manufacturing and sale of counterfeit liquor through a bar in Ibrahimpatnam. Investigators allege the operation was part of a larger conspiracy to embarrass the state's coalition government through illegal profits.

articles.headline: Banda recorded as India's hottest district reaching 48.2 degrees Celsius
articles.summary: Banda district in Uttar Pradesh recorded a maximum temperature of 48.2°C, making it the hottest location in India on Tuesday. Researchers attribute the extreme heat to years of ecological destruction, including large-scale mining and deforestation. The heat has forced the local administration to shut operations by 10 AM and led to a surge in equipment failures at power substations.

articles.headline: UP police bust AI-powered pornography racket targeting school students
articles.summary: Cyber police in Uttar Pradesh arrested a 28-year-old B.Tech graduate for running a Telegram network that used AI to create morphed explicit images. The network had nearly 7,000 subscribers, mostly students aged 12 to 17, who paid a monthly fee for access. Police seized devices containing thousands of lewd videos and traced financial transactions totaling ₹1.20 crore.

articles.headline: Army Chief hails Operation Sindoor as a coherent national act
articles.summary: General Upendra Dwivedi described Operation Sindoor, India's response to the Pahalgam terror strike, as a successful dismantling of terror infrastructure. The 88-hour high-intensity operation in May 2025 involved fighter jets and drones. Dwivedi emphasized that the deliberate halt of military action was a form of 'smart power' that converted a military moment into a strategic victory.

articles.headline: US seizes Iran-linked oil tanker Skywave in Indian Ocean
articles.summary: US officials seized the oil tanker Skywave in the Indian Ocean, alleging it was transporting over a million barrels of Iranian crude. The vessel had been sanctioned in March for its role in Iran's 'shadow fleet.' This enforcement action comes amid heightened tensions and US efforts to curb Iranian oil exports and disrupt its maritime revenue streams.

articles.headline: Automakers seek GST reductions for flex-fuel vehicles to drive adoption
articles.summary: Indian car manufacturers have asked the government for GST sops on flex-fuel vehicles, arguing that high-ethanol blends must be priced lower than petrol to attract buyers. Currently, these vehicles are taxed similarly to standard petrol cars. Auto firms suggest that lowering taxes on two-wheelers and fuel could accelerate the transition to sustainable energy in the transport sector.

articles.headline: TCS links performance pay to office attendance in updated policy
articles.summary: Tata Consultancy Services has revised its compensation structure to link monthly performance pay to work-from-office attendance and deployment metrics. Revised letters indicate that variable components, once paid quarterly, may now be adjusted based on organizational imperatives like physical office presence. The move has created confusion among employees regarding its impact on take-home pay.

articles.headline: Indian banks enhance cyber defenses to combat AI-driven security threats
articles.summary: Major Indian banks are increasing investment in cyber insurance and hiring specialist engineers to counter sophisticated AI-linked attacks. Following a finance ministry review, HDFC and Axis Bank are simulating 'adversarial AI' scenarios to test resilience. Concerns follow reports that advanced AI models can now identify system vulnerabilities faster than traditional human-led defenses.

articles.headline: Arsenal moves five points clear in title race after Burnley win
articles.summary: Arsenal secured a 1-0 victory over Burnley through a Kai Havertz header, moving five points clear at the top of the Premier League table. While Manchester City holds games in hand, Arsenal will be confirmed as champions if City fails to win their next match. Mikel Arteta’s side has now recorded four consecutive league wins without conceding a single goal.

articles.headline: Rishabh Pant lose Test vice-captaincy and spot in India's ODI squad
articles.summary: In a major leadership shift, the Indian selection committee stripped Rishabh Pant of his Test vice-captaincy and excluded him from the ODI squad for the Afghanistan series. KL Rahul has been named the new Test vice-captain. Chief selector Ajit Agarkar stated the decision aims to help Pant focus on becoming a world-class Test batter, while new bowling talent is being blooded for ODIs.

articles.headline: Rajasthan Royals defeat Lucknow Super Giants led by teen's ninety-three
articles.summary: Rajasthan Royals secured a crucial seven-wicket win over Lucknow Super Giants in Jaipur. Chasing a target of 221, 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashed 93 off 38 balls, including 10 sixes. The win keeps RR in the hunt for a playoff berth with 14 points. LSG's Mitch Marsh scored 96 in a losing cause as Sooryavanshi neared Chris Gayle's record for most sixes in a season.

articles.headline: Indian Corporate Profits Hit Record High in Fourth Quarter
articles.summary: The net profit margin for 837 sampled companies reached a record 11.3% in Q4 FY26. Total net profit rose 15.5% to ₹3.24 lakh crore, while revenue grew 9.5%. Profitability was driven by lower employee remuneration costs and reduced interest burdens. Banks and non-banking financial companies contributed significantly to these record earnings.

articles.headline: Government Prepared to Procure Fertilizers From 28 Countries
articles.summary: Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan stated that India is in contact with 28 countries to ensure adequate fertilizer supply for the upcoming Kharif season. The government has already approved ₹41,534 crore in nutrition-based subsidies to protect farmers from price volatility. He also urged farmers to adopt organic farming and drought-resistant crops.

articles.headline: GST Rules May Be Eased for E-commerce Dark Stores
articles.summary: The Finance Ministry is discussing with states to simplify GST registration for e-commerce and quick-commerce warehouses. Currently, adding every new 'place of business' requires cumbersome portal updates. The move aims to support rapid expansion in smaller cities. Experts suggest a centralized registration model within states for better efficiency.

articles.headline: API Holdings Focuses on Profitability and Debt Before IPO
articles.summary: API Holdings, the parent of Pharmeasy, is prioritizing debt reduction and profitability before launching its IPO. CEO Rahul Guha stated the company reduced debt from ₹1,800 crore to ₹1,050 crore in nine months. The company aims for a listing around 2027 once it reaches a sustainable profit and a near-zero debt balance sheet.

articles.headline: MakeMyTrip Operating Profit Rises 13% in FY26
articles.summary: Nasdaq-listed MakeMyTrip reported a 13% increase in adjusted operating profit to $18.88 million for FY26. Gross bookings reached a record $1,039.08 million during the year. The company attributed the performance to robust travel demand and increased efficiency in its travel service offerings.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Refuses SpiceJet More Time for Maran Payment
articles.summary: The Supreme Court directed SpiceJet to approach the Delhi High Court regarding its plea for a three-month extension to pay ₹144 crore to Kalanithi Maran. Senior Advocate Mukul Rohatgi argued that the West Asia crisis had impacted the airline's finances. The court noted that earlier financial relief packages from the government cannot be used as a basis for extension.

articles.headline: IndiGo Flight Passengers Stuck in Dark Due to Power Failure
articles.summary: About 160 passengers on an IndiGo flight from Vadodara to Delhi were stuck inside the aircraft for 30 minutes in the dark. The delay was caused by a technical failure in the Ground Power Unit (GPU) while the plane was on the tarmac. Passengers reported excessive heat as the air conditioning was non-functional during the outage.

articles.headline: Dr. Reddy's Targets Global Markets With Semaglutide and Biosimilars
articles.summary: Dr. Reddy's Laboratories is preparing to enter the global Semaglutide (generic Ozempic) market by 2026 as production constraints ease. CEO Erez Israeli stated the company is also focusing on Oncology and peptides to drive future growth. The firm aims to surpass traditional generic drugs in the US market through innovation in specialized therapeutic areas.

articles.headline: SME IPO Market Regains Momentum With 13 Expected Listings
articles.summary: After a two-month slowdown, the SME IPO market is recovering with 13 listings expected in May. FY26 saw a record 255 SME listings, up from 235 in FY25. Market experts believe the primary market gap has encouraged small firms. SEBI and exchanges have recently tightened audit and profit criteria to ensure higher quality among listing companies.

articles.headline: Mutual Funds Reduce IT Sector Holdings to Eight-Year Low
articles.summary: Mutual fund exposure to the IT sector dropped to 6.7% in April 2026, the lowest in eight years. Fund managers are cautious due to slowing global demand and risks associated with Artificial Intelligence. SBI Mutual Fund's allocation to IT was 5.9%. Conversely, UTI MF maintains a higher holding at 15.7% despite the broader industry shift.

articles.headline: Indian Markets End Flat as IT Gains Offset Bank Losses
articles.summary: The Sensex fell 0.15% to 75,200.85 while the Nifty slipped 0.14% to 23,618. A 3.2% jump in the IT index provided support, but losses in major banks like HDFC and ICICI dragged the benchmarks down. Investors remained cautious regarding the West Asia conflict and US Federal Reserve policy cues. Adani Group shares showed mixed results.

articles.headline: US Extends Sanctions Waiver for Russian Oil Purchases
articles.summary: The US Treasury has extended a waiver allowing the purchase of Russian crude oil and petroleum products loaded before April 17 until June 17. This provides relief to India, which currently imports 1.87 million barrels per day from Russia, accounting for 40% of its total oil imports. The extension helps stabilize the physical market for energy-sensitive nations.

articles.headline: Axis Bank to Open 500 New Branches in FY27
articles.summary: Axis Bank plans to add 500 new branches in the current fiscal year, focusing on senior citizens and NRIs. Currently, the bank has 6,275 branches. Around 40% of new branches will be in metros and 60% in non-metropolitan areas. The bank is investing 9-10% of its operating budget into technology and AI for improved underwriting and customer profiling.

articles.headline: India Ratings Forecasts 6.7% GDP Growth for FY27
articles.summary: India Ratings and Research expects India's GDP to grow at 6.7% in FY27, with retail inflation averaging 4.4%. The agency predicts the RBI will maintain the repo rate at 5.25% throughout the year. The report noted that while crude oil prices pose a risk, inflation is likely to remain within the central bank's target range.

articles.headline: EPFO to Launch WhatsApp Chatbot for Member Queries
articles.summary: Labour Minister Mansukh Mandaviya announced that the Employees' Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) will launch a WhatsApp-based chatbot. The automated 24/7 service will allow members to check account details and requests in regional languages. The initiative aims to reduce the burden on physical helpdesks and call centers.

articles.headline: Credit Card Spending Surges in Small and Medium Cities
articles.summary: An SBI Card report reveals that 77% of UPI-active credit card customers and 81% of such spending now come from non-metro cities. Total retail spending reached a record ₹3.54 lakh crore in FY26, a 15% increase. Online transactions now account for 62.5% of total retail credit card spending, driven by digital awareness and expanding payment infrastructure.

articles.headline: Sunil Mittal Announces 10-Year Succession Plan for Bharti Airtel
articles.summary: Bharti Airtel Chairman Sunil Mittal (68) has announced a decade-long leadership transition plan. The strategy involves consolidating shareholding under Bharti Telecom to remain the sole controlling entity. Mittal's children, Shravin, Kavin, and Esha, are involved in various arms of the group, but professional management like CEO Gopal Vittal will continue to run core operations.

articles.headline: India and Gulf Nations Seek Deep Strategic Security Partnership
articles.summary: Analysis suggests India and Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) countries should move beyond a buyer-seller relationship toward a deep strategic partnership. This would involve naval coordination, drone defense, and cybersecurity. The GCC is India's largest regional trade group, accounting for 15.4% of total trade and over 60% of crude oil needs in FY25.

articles.headline: India Semiconductor Mission Phase 2 May Extend to 12 Years
articles.summary: The government is considering extending the duration of the India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) second phase from 5 years to 12 years. The extension aims to provide long-term support for MSMEs in the global supply chain and improve chip design capabilities. The second phase could see a total investment of ₹1.5 lakh crore, focusing on raw materials and packaging units.

articles.headline: Bihar and Madhya Pradesh Outperform National GDP Growth Average
articles.summary: A report by Client Associates shows that lower-income states like Bihar and Madhya Pradesh are showing structural economic shifts. Bihar recorded a nominal GDP growth of 14.97% over five years, exceeding the national average of 14.1%. Despite having the lowest per capita income at ₹69,321, the state is attracting investment in ports, data centers, and infrastructure.

articles.headline: Nordic Nations Pledge $100 Billion Investment in India
articles.summary: During the India-Nordic Summit in Oslo, Norway, Denmark, Finland, Sweden, and Iceland committed to investing $100 billion in India over the next 15 years. The investment is expected to create 1 million jobs. PM Modi invited Nordic defense firms to utilize 100% FDI in Indian defense corridors and proposed deeper cooperation in green technology and the blue economy.

articles.headline: Big Bash League Considers Matches in Chennai to Woo Indian Fans
articles.summary: Cricket Australia is exploring the possibility of hosting Big Bash League (BBL) matches at Chennai's MA Chidambaram Stadium. The move aims to tap into the Indian market for television and digital revenue after previous attempts to sell franchise stakes failed. TNCA officials confirmed that preliminary discussions for hosting early-season matches are underway.

articles.headline: IPL Franchise Valuations Projected to Reach $15 Billion by 2032
articles.summary: A report by Hurun India projects that average IPL franchise valuations will reach $15 billion by 2032, rivaling the US NFL. Currently, the average value stands at $1.8 billion. KKR, Mumbai Indians, and Chennai Super Kings are the highest-valued teams. Individual player earnings also hit records, with Virat Kohli earning ₹230.2 crore over 18 seasons.

articles.headline: Governance uncertainty leads to ₹10 lakh crore loss for Tata stocks
articles.summary: Listed Tata Group companies have lost nearly ₹10 lakh crore in market value since Ratan Tata's death in October 2024. The combined market capitalization of 21 companies fell 28.7% to ₹24.4 lakh crore by March 2026. This decline is attributed to succession-related uncertainty and governance tussles at Tata Trusts and Tata Sons, causing foreign portfolio investors to pare their stakes.

articles.headline: Fuel prices hiked for second time in a week by OMCs
articles.summary: Public sector oil marketing companies raised retail prices for petrol and diesel by approximately 90 paise per litre on Tuesday. This marks the second increase in less than a week, following a ₹3 per litre hike on May 15. In New Delhi, petrol prices rose to ₹98.64 and diesel to ₹91.58. The hikes follow higher global crude costs and exchange rate volatility.

articles.headline: Closure of US fraud investigations provides relief to Adani Group
articles.summary: US authorities have withdrawn criminal securities and wire fraud charges against Gautam Adani and his nephew Sagar Adani in a New York court. The Department of Justice decided to stop the prosecution after reviewing the case. This follows a civil settlement with the SEC regarding 2021 bond disclosures. The move is seen as significant relief for the group's global fundraising plans.

articles.headline: BPCL Q4 net profit jumps 28% to ₹5,625 crore
articles.summary: Bharat Petroleum Corporation reported a 28% year-on-year increase in consolidated net profit for Q4 FY26, despite an impairment loss of ₹4,349 crore related to its subsidiary Bharat Petro Resources. For the full fiscal year 2025-26, the company's net profit rose 93.78% to ₹25,843 crore. BPCL achieved its highest-ever refinery throughput of 41.15 million tonnes during the year.

articles.headline: BMW India announces third price hike of 2026 due to forex
articles.summary: BMW Group India plans to increase vehicle prices by 2-3% in its third hike this year, citing foreign exchange volatility and logistics challenges. Despite price increases, the company saw 17% year-on-year growth in Q1 2026. Electric vehicles now constitute 26% of their sales. The company launched the MINI Cooper S GP Inspired Edition in Surat at ₹58.9 lakh.

articles.headline: Zydus Lifesciences Q4 profit rises 8.7% to ₹1,272 crore
articles.summary: Zydus Lifesciences reported an 8.7% increase in consolidated net profit for the fourth quarter, reaching ₹1,272 crore. Earnings were impacted by a one-time settlement expense of ₹397.5 crore. Revenue from product sales grew 18% to ₹7,434 crore, driven by strong performance in consumer wellness and international formulations. The board approved a share buyback of up to ₹1,100 crore.

articles.headline: India's March petroleum product imports hit eight-year low
articles.summary: Disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz led to India's refined petroleum product imports falling to an average of 765,000 barrels per day in March 2026, the lowest since 2018. LPG imports were particularly affected, dropping significantly. Total product imports fell by 25.5% year-on-year in March. Crude oil imports also hit an eight-month low of 4.5 million barrels per day.

articles.headline: Indian exporters must meet strict quality standards under EU FTA
articles.summary: Additional Secretary Darpan Jain stated that Indian exporters must improve quality compliance to benefit from tariff eliminations in the upcoming India-EU Free Trade Agreement. The FTA is expected to be implemented next year, eliminating tariffs on 99% of Indian exports by value. Compliance with EU standards for human, animal, and plant health will be critical for textile and footwear sectors.

articles.headline: Zohran Mamdani meets Wall Street CEOs to discuss tax proposals
articles.summary: New York City Democratic Socialist politician Zohran Mamdani held individual meetings with JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon and Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon. The sessions follow Mamdani's proposals for higher taxes on the wealthy and a new levy on second homes. The meetings represent an effort to build relations with the business community despite policy differences.

articles.headline: SEBI clarifies ND-PMS clients can pledge shares for loans
articles.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) clarified that clients using non-discretionary portfolio management services (ND-PMS) have the right to pledge their securities as collateral for borrowing. Since beneficial ownership remains with the investor, such pledging does not violate regulations prohibiting portfolio managers from borrowing on behalf of clients.

articles.headline: SEBI proposes decentralized API-based model for STP framework
articles.summary: SEBI has proposed revamping the Straight-Through Processing (STP) framework by replacing the current centralized hub with a decentralized API-based connectivity model. The move aims to reduce costs, lower latency, and eliminate concentration risk, as 95-99% of messages currently route through a single provider. The change will not require modifications from end-users like stockbrokers.

articles.headline: Keto Motors debuts on BSE following reverse merger
articles.summary: Hyderabad-based commercial electric vehicle manufacturer Keto Motors listed on the BSE Tuesday after a reverse merger with Taaza International Ltd. Shareholders received three Taaza shares for every two Keto shares held. The company is focused on the ₹300 crore electric bus manufacturing project in Telangana and intends to expand into urban mobility networks.

articles.headline: Star Health targets ₹24,000 crore premium in FY27
articles.summary: Standalone health insurer Star Health targets a 18% topline growth in FY27, aiming for ₹24,000 crore in gross written premium. The company is benefiting from increased demand and improved customer retention following the GST waiver on health insurance. It launched 'Value Plus', an affordable product for tier 2-4 cities, and is processing 25% of claims using AI models.

articles.headline: RBI proposes sharper disclosure regime for banks under Basel III
articles.summary: The RBI has issued a consultation paper to improve the quality and consistency of bank disclosures. Lenders will be required to publish granular data on capital adequacy, liquidity, and risk weighted assets every quarter in a uniform format. Banks must create a dedicated 'Regulatory Disclosure Section' on their websites to host all Pillar 3 disclosures starting September 2026.

articles.headline: Hatsun Agro Product Q4 net profit up 18.6% to ₹51 crore
articles.summary: Hatsun Agro Product (HAP) reported a net profit of ₹51 crore for the quarter ending March 2026, up from ₹43 crore a year prior. Revenue from operations rose 14.6% to ₹2,580 crore. For the full financial year, PAT stood at ₹356 crore. The company successfully reduced its debt-to-equity ratio from 1.44 to 0.68 over the last two years through operational efficiency.

articles.headline: Central Bank of India to open GIFT City unit in June
articles.summary: Central Bank of India is set to operationalize its IFSC Banking Unit at GIFT City in Gandhinagar in the first week of next month. The bank has received necessary regulatory approvals from the Reserve Bank of India and the International Financial Services Centres Authority, according to MD and CEO Kalyan Kumar.

articles.headline: Edible oil inflation doubles as import dependence strains consumers
articles.summary: Refined oil inflation has more than doubled over the past four months, with mustard oil rising by 21 percentage points. India imports 60% of its edible oil requirements, spending $19.35 billion in FY26. PM Narendra Modi has appealed to the public to reduce consumption to lower the import bill and improve health. Structural weaknesses in domestic production remain a primary cause.

articles.headline: Apollo Tyres to invest ₹3,500 crore amid European restructuring
articles.summary: Apollo Tyres is moving ahead with a ₹3,500 crore expansion plan while closing its last manufacturing plant in the Netherlands on June 30. The company reported consolidated revenue of ₹7,340 crore for the March quarter. Most of the capital expenditure will be used for growth in India, where capacity utilization is at 90%. Natural rubber prices have risen to ₹250 per kg.

articles.headline: Piyush Goyal: Geopolitical risks are opportunities for faster reforms
articles.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal urged the Indian industry to view global economic uncertainties as opportunities for growth. Speaking at the ASsocham Reform Summit, he encouraged companies to adopt AI and improve efficiency to reduce waste. He stated the government is considering a single body at the industrial State level to improve ease of doing business and speed up approvals.

articles.headline: Shyam Metalics plans ₹10,000 crore investment in West Bengal
articles.summary: Shyam Metalics and Energy will invest ₹10,000 crore in West Bengal projects over the next three years. The plan includes a railway coach manufacturing facility and hot rolled coil production. The company expects to double its employment strength in the state to 50,000 people. The investment is contingent on new industrial and land policies from the state government.

articles.headline: Infosys finalises average Q4 performance bonus at 70%
articles.summary: Infosys has fixed the average performance bonus for the fourth quarter of FY26 at 70%, a decrease from the 85% paid in the previous quarter. Variable pay eligibility is linked to internal employee grading bands. Top performers at the PL4 level received up to 82%. The IT giant reported a 20.9% rise in Q4 net profit to ₹8,501 crore despite global macroeconomic uncertainty.

articles.headline: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang: Tech ecosystem has entered Era of Useful AI
articles.summary: At Dell Technologies World 2026, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang stated that agentic AI adoption is growing exponentially. Huang highlighted that computation requirements for AI have increased up to 1,000 times, and Nvidia is collaborating with Dell to build full-stack AI factories. He noted that enterprises now face the challenge of turning intelligence into impact at speed.

articles.headline: India and Vietnam deepen ties with AI and quantum tech deals
articles.summary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh and Vietnamese counterpart General Phan Van Giang reviewed their defence partnership in Hanoi. They virtually inaugurated a Language Lab and an AI Lab in Vietnam. The two nations signed an MoU to cooperate in the fields of artificial intelligence and quantum technology, marking a new chapter in their Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.

articles.headline: Government plans dedicated Basmati Development Board for exports
articles.summary: The government is considering establishing a statutory 'Basmati Development Board' to improve farmer incomes and manage seed quality. The move follows demands from rice exporter associations and farmer groups who claim that premium prices for Basmati have eroded despite rising input costs. The board would be separate from the control of Apeda to provide a niche focus on the commodity.

articles.headline: Jewellers encourage gold recycling to curb imports as prices surge
articles.summary: Major Indian jewellers are launching initiatives to encourage customers to exchange old ornaments for new designs to minimize gold imports. Tanishq’s 'OldGoldNewIndia' campaign saw 4.4 lakh customers recycle over 10,000 kg of gold. This shift follows PM Modi’s appeal to view gold as a renewable resource and use the Gold Monetisation Scheme to unlock dormant household reserves.

articles.headline: Nepal's duty-free refined oil exports to India surge 113%
articles.summary: India's edible oil imports from Nepal under the SAFTA arrangement rose 113% to 7.36 lakh tonnes in FY26. Refined soybean oil accounted for the majority of these shipments. The surge in zero-duty imports from Nepal contributed significantly to India's overall 3% increase in edible oil imports, despite a sharp depreciation of the rupee and domestic supply efforts.

articles.headline: ITC emerges as India's leading organic spice exporter in 2025
articles.summary: ITC Ltd has become India's top exporter of organic spices, supporting over 31,800 farmers across 2.2 lakh acres. The company's processing facility in Guntur, one of Asia's largest, handles over 15 spice varieties with a capacity of 20,000 metric tonnes annually. The project uses barcode-based sourcing for end-to-end traceability and aims to improve sustainable agricultural livelihoods.

articles.headline: Top five States drive 48% of India's national GDP
articles.summary: Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu, Uttar Pradesh, Karnataka, and Gujarat contributed nearly half of India's national GDP in FY25, according to a report by Client Associates. Maharashtra leads with a 13.3% share. The report highlights deep regional disparities, with Maharashtra's economy being 133 times larger than Mizoram's, and Bihar's per capita income being eight times lower than Sikkim's.

articles.headline: EPFO 3.0 to launch UPI payouts for subscribers
articles.summary: The Ministry of Labour is preparing to allow 7.48 crore active EPFO subscribers to withdraw up to 75% of their provident fund balance directly into bank accounts via UPI. The EPFO 3.0 reform aims to digitize records and expedite claim processing. Pending litigation cases in the EPFO have dropped to a record low of 27,639, following mission-mode drives to resolve long-standing disputes.

articles.headline: Trade and digital connectivity weaponised into coercion: Army Chief
articles.summary: Army Chief General Upendra Dwivedi stated that global trade and supply chains are now being used as 'instruments of coercion' rather than shields against war. Speaking at a CLAWS seminar, he insisted that security is a precondition for prosperity. He highlighted that the boundary between security and prosperity has blurred and that speed of innovation is now a critical strategic variable.

articles.headline: Chemists launch 24-hour strike against online pharmacy discounts
articles.summary: Over 12 lakh chemists across India began a 24-hour strike starting Tuesday midnight to protest predatory pricing by online pharmacies. The All India Organisation of Chemists and Druggists (AIOCD) claims that deep discounts from e-commerce entities threaten the survival of small medical stores. They are demanding stronger regulations and the withdrawal of specific government notifications.

articles.headline: Global AI spending to reach $2.59 trillion in 2026
articles.summary: Worldwide AI spending is forecast to hit $2.59 trillion in 2026, a 47% year-over-year increase, according to Gartner. Investments are shifting from applications to infrastructure, including chips, cloud capacity, and data centres. AI-optimised servers are expected to become the largest market segment, as cloud providers expand capacity for generative AI and agentic workflows.

articles.headline: Elon Musk loses fraud lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman
articles.summary: A federal jury in California rejected Elon Musk's claims that OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman betrayed the company's non-profit mission by becoming a for-profit business. The jury found that Musk waited too long to sue. The verdict marks a conclusion to a high-profile legal battle between the co-founders. Musk's lawyers have vowed to appeal the decision.

articles.headline: RIL, NTPC, Adani & Bajaj Group to Invest ₹6.5Lcr in Nuclear Power
articles.summary: Major Indian conglomerates including Reliance Industries, NTPC, Adani Power, and the Bajaj Group are planning to invest a combined ₹6.5 lakh crore in the nuclear energy sector. The investments, focused on Maharashtra, aim to add 25.4 GW of power generation capacity and create 123,000 jobs. The move comes as the government encourages private sector participation in the nuclear power roadmap.

articles.headline: Petrol and Diesel Prices Hike by 90 Paise per Litre
articles.summary: State-run fuel retailers in India have increased the prices of petrol and diesel by about 90 paise per litre, marking the second hike in less than a week. The price adjustment comes amid rising crude oil prices and a weakening rupee. Petrol prices in Delhi have risen to ₹98.64 per litre, while diesel is now priced at ₹91.58, adding to inflationary pressures for consumers and businesses.

articles.headline: Rating Agencies Peg Q4 GDP Growth at 6.7-7% Amid Global Turmoil
articles.summary: Leading rating agencies and economists have forecasted India's GDP growth for the fourth quarter (Q4) of FY26 at 6.7% to 7%. The projections reflect resilience despite global headwinds, including the West Asia conflict and fluctuating oil prices. For the full fiscal year 2026, growth is expected to remain robust, though slightly lower than earlier highs due to external economic pressures.

articles.headline: India Peak Power Demand Hits Record High of 260.5 GW
articles.summary: India’s peak power demand reached a record 260.5 GW on Monday as heat waves intensified across large parts of the country. This spike, occurring even before the peak summer month of June, has prompted the government to enhance power generation capacity and ensure coal stocks. Authorities are monitoring the grid closely to manage the surge in consumption driven by cooling requirements.

articles.headline: Education Minister Reviews NEET Preparations Following Cancellation of May 3 Exam
articles.summary: Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan chaired a meeting to review preparations for the upcoming NEET re-examination after the May 3 exam was cancelled due to paper leak allegations. The minister emphasized the need for foolproof security protocols and a secure examination process. The National Testing Agency (NTA) has been directed to ensure transparency and integrity in the conduct.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Refuses to Recall Order on Stray Dog Management
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India has refused to recall its earlier directions regarding the management and relocation of stray dogs. The court maintained that state authorities must take measures to ensure public safety while adhering to animal welfare laws. The bench emphasized that authorities should balance the rights of citizens and animals by implementing effective sterilization programs.

articles.headline: Delhi High Court Issues Notice to Kejriwal in Defamation Case
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has issued notices to Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal and other AAP leaders in a defamation case filed by a BJP leader. The case pertains to alleged vilifying remarks made by the AAP leaders against the judiciary. The court has directed the respondents to file their replies within a specified timeframe as it proceeds with the legal examination of the allegations.

articles.headline: CBI Arrests Army Colonel for Allegedly Accepting ₹50 Lakh Bribe
articles.summary: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has arrested a Colonel from the Army Ordnance Corps for allegedly accepting a bribe of ₹50 lakh. The arrest was made in connection with irregularities in defense tenders and contracts. The CBI is conducting further searches and investigations to identify other officials and middlemen involved in the alleged corruption scandal within the department.

articles.headline: PM Modi Hails New Era of India-Nordic Strategic Ties
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has stated that the signing of trade and economic partnership agreements marks the beginning of a 'golden era' in India-Nordic relations. Following a summit with Nordic leaders, Modi highlighted potential cooperation in green technology, innovation, and sustainable development. The partnership is expected to attract significant investment and boost bilateral trade.

articles.headline: Goyal Urges Industry to Boost Productivity and Reduce Waste
articles.summary: Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal has urged Indian businesses to focus on increasing productivity and reducing waste to remain competitive globally. Speaking at a business forum, Goyal emphasized that industry should not panic over global crises but instead find opportunities to innovate and capture new markets. He highlighted the importance of indigenous manufacturing and efficiency.

articles.headline: DGCA to Monitor Boeing Fuel-Switch Tests After London Incident
articles.summary: India's aviation regulator, DGCA, will monitor sensitive fuel-switch tests on Boeing aircraft following an incident during a flight from Seattle to London. The tests are aimed at ensuring the reliability of fuel control systems and preventing technical glitches during critical flight phases. The regulator has directed airlines to follow strict protocols and report any anomalies immediately.

articles.headline: Vladimir Putin to Visit China for Strategic Talks with Xi Jinping
articles.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin is scheduled to visit China for a high-level meeting with President Xi Jinping. The visit is expected to focus on deepening the strategic partnership between the two nations, discussing regional conflicts, and exploring new avenues for economic cooperation. The meeting comes at a time of significant global geopolitical shifts and heightened tensions with the West.

articles.headline: Standard Chartered to Cut 8,000 Jobs in Global AI Transition
articles.summary: Standard Chartered plans to cut approximately 8,000 jobs globally as part of its strategy to integrate artificial intelligence and automate back-office operations. The move is intended to reduce costs and improve efficiency across its international network. The bank stated that the transition would allow it to focus more on high-value roles while adapting to the evolving digital banking landscape.

articles.headline: Karnataka Bank Q4 Net Profit Jumps 62% to ₹408 Crore
articles.summary: Karnataka Bank has reported a significant 62% increase in its net profit to ₹408 crore for the fourth quarter ended March 2026. The growth was driven by higher interest income and improved asset quality. The bank's board has also recommended a dividend for shareholders, reflecting its strong financial performance and positive outlook for the upcoming fiscal year.

articles.headline: Former Uttarakhand CM Bhuwan Chandra Khanduri dies in Dehradun aged 91
articles.summary: Major General (retd) Bhuwan Chandra Khanduri, a former two-time Chief Minister of Uttarakhand and senior BJP leader, passed away Tuesday after a prolonged illness. A decorated Army officer and former Union Minister, Khanduri was instrumental in the execution of the Golden Quadrilateral highway project. The state has declared three days of mourning for the veteran leader.

articles.headline: Indian Rupee hits all-time low of 96.61 against US Dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee reached a record low of 96.61 against the dollar on Tuesday, ending at 96.53. The currency has weakened by 7.5% in 2026 due to persistent foreign fund outflows, elevated crude oil prices at $110/barrel, and a strengthening dollar index. Analysts suggest the currency could drift toward 97 unless global energy tensions ease or foreign capital returns.

articles.headline: PepsiCo India to invest ₹5,700 crore in manufacturing by 2030
articles.summary: PepsiCo India has committed to investing ₹5,700 crore by 2030 to ramp up its manufacturing capacity. New facilities are planned in Madhya Pradesh and Assam, along with a recent land purchase in Tamil Nadu. The company reported a revenue of ₹9,798 crore in CY25, representing an 8.3% growth. The investment aims to tap into India's growth opportunity in snacks and beverages.

articles.headline: Government promotes surface coal gasification with ₹37,500 crore scheme
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet has approved a massive ₹37,500-crore incentive scheme for surface coal gasification projects. The initiative aims to utilize India's 400 billion tonnes of coal reserves for cleaner energy and chemical production, including synthetic gas (Syngas) for urea and hydrogen. The goal is to gasify 100 million tonnes of coal by 2030 to reduce reliance on volatile fuel imports.
